english: groanings Pth.; sdań-bai dgrá-la yyag ltar k̥un (sdang ba'i dgra la g.yag ltar khun) he groans (grunts, bellows) like a yak against a fierce enemy Mil.

pf. k̥ums (khums) (cf. sk̥úm-pa (skhum pa)) to shrink, to be contracted, e.g. of the limbs, by gout; *yúr-ra rań-źin k̥úm-će yin* (*yur ra rang zhin khum ce yin*) W. the ditch will get narrower of itself; k̥úms-pa (khums pa) shrunk, shrivelled, contracted; fig. reduced, restricted, deprived of power.
Lex. and Cs. to comprehend, understand; Sch. also: to practise, to impress on the mind.

I. sbst. = k̥úr-ba (khur ba), pastry. II. vb., pf. and fut. k̥ur (khur), rarely bkúr (bkur) (v. bkúr-ba (bkur ba)) 1. to carry, convey, k̥ur(-ru) k̥úr-ba (khur (ru) khur ba) to carry a load; mi t̥ég-par k̥úr-ba (mi theg par khur ba) Med. to carry too heavy loads, prop. to carry what one cannot carry; *k̥ur śog* (*khur shog*) C., *k̥ur kyon* (*khur kyon*) W. bring! fetch! *k̥ur soń* (*khur song*) C., *k̥ur kyer* (*khur khyer*) W. take away! carry off! k̥úr-du tógs-te (khur du thogs te) taking up in order to carry, taking on one's back Dzl.; *lág-par k̥úr-će* (*lag par khur ce*) W. to hold in one's hand. — 2. to carry away or off, ro k̥úr-ba (ro khur ba) Pth., to carry away a corpse; to take along with, in W. even: to pocket, *sém-la k̥úr-će* (*sems la khur ce*) W. to take to heart; *'á-ne k̥úr-će* (*a ne khur ce*) to take a wife, to marry. — k̥úr-t̥ag (khur thag) carrying-girth, rope or strap Thgy. Cf. k̥ur (khur), k̥úr-ba (khur ba) etc.

to subdue, to subject Cs.; Sch. also: to be uneasy about. Lex.: yyóg-tu k̥úl-ba (g.yog tu khul ba); v. also k̥ul (khul) 3. Zam.; kral k̥úl-ba (khral khul ba) perh. to force a tax, a rate, on a person.

pf. k̥egs (khegs) to hinder, stop, shut off, debar, lam (lam) the way Mil.; ji-ltar bkag ruń ma k̥égs-pas (ji ltar bkag rung ma khegs pas) Mil., although they prohibited, tried to prob. him), he was not prohibited; rgyál-bai p̥yág-gis kyań mi k̥égs-pa (rgyal ba'i phyag gis kyang mi khegs pa) Mil. not being hindered even by Buddha's power.
pf. k̥eńs (khengs), to be full, ćus k̥eńs yod-pa (chus khengs yod pa) Glr.; blo-grós ma k̥éńs-te (blo gros ma khengs te) his mind not being satiated Tar. 135, 13.

pf. k̥ebs (khebs), to cover, to spread over, yońs-su k̥ébs-té (yongs su khebs te) being covered all over Sty.; k̥a tams-ćad k̥ebs-te (kha thams cad khebs te) over the whole face Sty.; to overshadow Dzl. 82, 17.

pf. k̥el (khel) 1. to put on, to load, to pack on, = gél-ba ('gel ba); bću-t̥óg k̥él-na (bcu thog khel na) when the ten stories or lofts shall have been put on Mil.nt. 2. to spin, = k̥ál-ba ('khal ba) C., Glr.

(vb. to mk̥ó-ba (mkho ba)), to wish, to want, to think useful, serviceable, necessary, to have occasion for Mil.; k̥ó-ste (kho ste) ,on he will be able to make use of it Mil.; *k̥óa mi k̥o* (*kho a mi kho*) or *k̥ó-će med* (*kho ce med*) W. I do not want it, I do not like it; k̥o-bjéd (kho byed) fit for use, useful.
weak from old age, decrepit, decayed; rgan (rgan) or rgas-k̥ógs (rgas khogs) id.; sńo-kóg (sngo kog), skya-kóy (skya koy) (sic) Thgy. with a complexion blue or pale from old age.
(cf. sgóń-ba (sgong ba)) to contract one's limbs, to sit in a cowering position, to squat; to hide one's self; dpa k̥óń-ba (dpa' khong ba) to become discouraged, disheartened Thgy.

1. surface, superficies; sai k̥od snyóms-pa (sa'i khod snyoms pa) to remove inequalities of the surface, to level, to plane, k̥ód-snyoms-pa (khod snyoms pa) levelled, made even, plain; also fig.: bár-gyi k̥od snyoms (bar gyi khod snyoms) gaps were filled up, i.e. distinctions of rank, wealth etc. were done away with, not in consequence of a revolution, but as an act of kindness, forced upon the people by a despotic government. — 2. a mill-stone, *yá-k̥o* (*ya kho*) the upper stone, *má-k̥o* (*ma kho*) the nether stone C.

to sit down, to sit; bar-snáń-la (bar snang la), suspended in the air, floating, soaring, frq. of gods and saints in legends; rgyal-srid-la (rgyal srid la) to have been raised to the throne Tar.; to live, to dwell Dzl.; gen. used like a passive to gód-pa ('god pa) to be put, placed, established (in virtue, in a doctrine, = to be converted to); glegs-bám-du (glegs bam du) to be put down in writing, to be recorded Tar. Cf. k̥ád-pa (khad pa).
